title: asclepias viridiflora
intro: Asclepias viridiflora, also known as Green Antelopehorn Milkweed, is a perennial plant species in the milkweed family that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to induce vomiting and as a laxative, due to the presence of cardenolides and other glycosides. The plant has been reported to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Asclepias viridiflora can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes.
